chalkboard.content:
## Current State

**Book**: Drafting chapters on memory systems and the Mirror pattern. James working with Claude on structure. Key tension: how much technical detail vs. narrative.

**Dominos**: Alex exploring competitive strategy pivot. Revenue flat but user engagement up 12%. Next board meeting March 15.

**TrueUp**: Brian's ops reports show Q1 pipeline healthy. Three enterprise deals in negotiation. Burn rate stable.

**Kai**: Started multiplication tables this week. Aaron helping with homework routine. Kai asked about "what computers think about" — potential book anecdote.

**Health**: Resting heart rate trending down (58 avg). Sleep consistency improved after setting consistent wake time. 3 runs this week.

memories.content: Discussed the Mirror pattern chapter with Claude. The key insight is that AI memory isn't about storage — it's about projection. Every time you ask an AI to remember something, you're really asking it to rebuild a world model. This is fundamentally different from human episodic memory. We landed on the metaphor of a chalkboard that gets erased and redrawn, vs. a filing cabinet.
memories.summary: Mirror chapter: memory as projection, not storage. Chalkboard metaphor.

memories.content: Outlined the companion site structure. Each chapter gets a reference page. Memory Viewer will be the interactive demo for the Octopus chapter.
memories.summary: Companion site structure — Memory Viewer as chapter demo.

memories.content: Revised the chapter ordering. Moved the Conductor pattern earlier because it sets up the idea of orchestration that the Mirror chapter depends on. Kill Your Darlings stays as chapter 3.
memories.summary: Chapter reorder: Conductor moved earlier, before Mirror.

memories.content: James noted that the book needs more personal stories grounding each pattern. Not just 'here's how AI works' but 'here's the moment I realized this mattered.' The Kai naming story is the prototype for this approach.
memories.summary: Book needs more personal grounding — Kai story as prototype.

memories.content: Alex presented the competitive analysis for Dominos. Main competitor launched a subscription tier. Alex wants to respond with a freemium model rather than matching subscriptions. Board is split. Need data on conversion funnels before March 15 meeting.
memories.summary: Dominos competitive response: freemium vs. subscription. Board split.

memories.content: Dominos user engagement up 12% month-over-month. Daily active users growing but revenue per user declining. Alex attributes this to the free tier being too generous.
memories.summary: Dominos: engagement +12%, but revenue per user declining.

memories.content: Alex asked ChatGPT to model three pricing scenarios for Dominos. Scenario B (freemium with usage caps) showed best projected LTV. Shared spreadsheet with the team.
memories.summary: Pricing model: freemium + usage caps best projected LTV.

memories.content: Dominos board meeting prep. Alex reviewing pitch deck. Key ask: approval for freemium pivot and $200K marketing budget for Q2.
memories.summary: Board meeting prep: freemium pivot + $200K Q2 marketing ask.

memories.content: Brian's weekly ops report for TrueUp. Pipeline: 3 enterprise deals in late stage (combined ARR $1.2M). Burn rate: $180K/month, 14 months runway. Team: hired senior backend engineer, still looking for a PM. Churn: down to 3.1% from 4.2% last quarter.
memories.summary: TrueUp ops: 3 enterprise deals, $180K burn, churn down to 3.1%.

memories.content: Brian flagged a potential issue with the TrueUp data pipeline. One enterprise prospect wants SOC2 compliance before signing. Brian estimating 6-8 weeks to complete if they start now.
memories.summary: TrueUp: enterprise prospect needs SOC2, 6-8 week timeline.

memories.content: TrueUp Q1 revenue tracking at $340K, slightly ahead of plan. Brian wants to accelerate hiring if Deal #2 closes this month.
memories.summary: TrueUp Q1 revenue $340K, ahead of plan. Hiring conditional on Deal #2.

memories.content: Brian shared a draft investor update for TrueUp. Tone is confident but honest about the SOC2 gap. James suggested emphasizing the churn improvement as the lead metric.
memories.summary: TrueUp investor update draft — lead with churn improvement.

memories.content: Kai had a great week at school. Multiplication tables clicking — went from struggling with 7s and 8s to getting 90% on the timed test. Aaron set up a reward chart that seems to be working. Kai also asked 'do computers think about things when they're turned off?' which led to a 20-minute conversation about sleep vs. shutdown.
memories.summary: Kai: multiplication breakthrough, 90% on timed test. Asked about computer consciousness.

memories.content: Kai's parent-teacher conference notes. Teacher says Kai is reading above grade level and shows strong creative thinking. Suggested enrichment programs for next year. Social skills good — has a solid friend group.
memories.summary: Parent-teacher conference: above grade level reading, strong creative thinking.

memories.content: Kai drew a picture of 'a robot that remembers everything' after overhearing James talk about the book. Aaron photographed it for the diary. Potential book illustration?
memories.summary: Kai drew 'a robot that remembers everything' — potential book illustration.

memories.content: Resting heart rate this week: 58 avg (down from 62 last month). Sleep score averaging 82. Three runs completed: 3.1mi, 4.2mi, 3.5mi. Weight stable at 175. Blood pressure 118/76.
memories.summary: Health: RHR 58 (down from 62), sleep score 82, three runs this week.

memories.content: Sleep consistency improved significantly after setting 6:30 AM alarm every day including weekends. Average sleep duration: 7.2 hours. Only one night below 6.5 hours this week.
memories.summary: Sleep consistency up with fixed 6:30 AM wake time. Avg 7.2 hrs.

memories.content: Completed longest run of the month: 4.2 miles at 9:12/mi pace. Felt strong. Considering signing up for a 10K in April.
memories.summary: Longest run: 4.2mi at 9:12 pace. Considering April 10K.

memories.content: Discussed with Claude how the Octopus memory system should handle contradictions — when two memories about the same entity conflict. Landed on: keep both, flag the conflict, let the chalkboard rebuild process resolve it by preferring recency + source trust. This is analogous to how human memory works: you don't delete old memories, you just update your working model.
memories.summary: Octopus contradiction handling: keep both, flag, resolve on rebuild.

memories.content: Weekly review: feeling good about momentum on the book, Kai is thriving, health metrics all trending right direction. Main stress point is the Dominos board meeting — want to make sure Alex is prepared. TrueUp is in a good groove with Brian driving ops.
memories.summary: Weekly review: book momentum good, Kai thriving, health improving. Watch: Dominos board prep.
